title: Build and Ship an Accessible Maze Solver with Python 3.14 and Tkinter
description: Python and data-structure learners who need to model, generate, solve, animate, test, package, playtest, and defend a complete accessible desktop maze application rather than copy one recursive tutorial. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; tcl and tk 8.6 behavior exposed by tkinter; event-driven lifecycle and thread boundary; grid identity and geometry; reciprocal wall models; canvas items, tags, layers, resize, scaling, input and focus; graph topology; seeded backtracking, randomized prim and kruskal, wilson loop-erased walks and distribution claims; dfs, bfs, dijkstra, a-star and solver evidence; cancellable after-based animation; explicit application states; pure-core architecture; accessible visual and structured-text puzzle experience; replay and safe json state; layered property, mutation, headless, gui and play testing; profiling and capacity; pyinstaller 6.21 platform-native packaging; local security and privacy; release, migration, rollback, recovery, support, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python evidence using original fixed fixtures plus explicit tcl interpreter, tk widget, display, input, assistive-technology, filesystem, native timing, package, accessibility playtest, performance, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic maze deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-platform defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves player goal, repository revision, Python and Tcl/Tk versions, grid and coordinate identity, seed, topology, algorithm state, action and event trace, accessibility requirement, bounded work, changed-case test, failure, recovery, and explicit browser-versus-desktop limits before adding one maze boundary.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, Canvas, windows, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, installers, display scaling, platform timing, or packaged release behavior; those require controlled native evidence.

Passing work requires stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate graph or state evidence, exact observable output, a changed and rejected case, a test that detects a deliberate defect, accessible player evidence, and a named owner for remaining risk.

competencies.statement: Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.
misconceptions: A window containing a grid is a complete player outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.

competencies.statement: Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.
misconceptions: A small learning project does not need repository or dependency evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.

competencies.statement: Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.
misconceptions: Accessibility can be added after the maze looks attractive.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.

competencies.statement: Separate pure-model, algorithm, Tcl interpreter, Tk widget, display, input, accessibility, package, and player evidence.
misconceptions: A solved screenshot proves correctness and usability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate pure-model, algorithm, Tcl interpreter, Tk widget, display, input, accessibility, package, and player evidence.

competencies.statement: State which claims browser models can establish and which require a controlled native desktop transfer.
misconceptions: Pyodide behavior proves Tkinter behavior on every desktop.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State which claims browser models can establish and which require a controlled native desktop transfer.

competencies.statement: Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.
misconceptions: Tkinter is a Python-drawn GUI with no Tcl runtime boundary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.

competencies.statement: Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.
misconceptions: Every dialog should create its own independent Tk root.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.

competencies.statement: Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.
misconceptions: A long callback is harmless because the interface has a mainloop.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.

competencies.statement: Explain tkinter thread routing and keep UI mutation on the owning event-loop path.
misconceptions: Any Python thread can safely update a widget directly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain tkinter thread routing and keep UI mutation on the owning event-loop path.

competencies.statement: Record Python, Tcl, Tk, operating system, display, scaling, and startup or shutdown evidence without overgeneralizing.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record Python, Tcl, Tk, operating system, display, scaling, and startup or shutdown evidence without overgeneralizing.

competencies.statement: Represent stable row-column identity separately from storage offset, graph vertex, pixel rectangle, and display label.
misconceptions: A pixel coordinate is a stable cell identifier.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent stable row-column identity separately from storage offset, graph vertex, pixel rectangle, and display label.

competencies.statement: Convert between row-column, linear index, logical point, and Canvas coordinates with explicit origins and axis direction.
misconceptions: Cartesian and Canvas y coordinates increase in the same direction.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert between row-column, linear index, logical point, and Canvas coordinates with explicit origins and axis direction.

competencies.statement: Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.
misconceptions: Numbers used in geometry are interchangeable when magnitudes look plausible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.

competencies.statement: Derive half-open cell bounds and shared edges so every viewport point has an intentional ownership rule.
misconceptions: Independent rounding of every corner cannot create gaps or overlaps.

competencies.statement: Test round trips, adjacency, boundary ownership, positive sizes, and changed viewport dimensions.

competencies.statement: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.
misconceptions: Four unrelated booleans cannot contradict each other.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.

competencies.statement: Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.
misconceptions: Changing the current cell wall is sufficient to connect two cells.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.

competencies.statement: Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.
misconceptions: A perfect maze must keep every outside edge closed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.

competencies.statement: Keep maze topology independent from Canvas item identifiers, colors, widths, and animation state.

competencies.statement: Reconcile cell count, neighbor pairs, reciprocal openings, boundary exceptions, and illegal diagonal links.

competencies.statement: Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.

competencies.statement: Define wall, terrain, solution, focus, annotation, and status layers with stable stacking rules.

competencies.statement: Use semantic tags for groups and stable model keys rather than retaining fragile positional ID lists.

competencies.statement: Choose full, regional, or item-diff redraw from measured complexity while preventing duplicate artifacts.

competencies.statement: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.

competencies.statement: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.

competencies.statement: Derive cell size and offsets from current available space, grid dimensions, minimum target, and aspect policy.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive cell size and offsets from current available space, grid dimensions, minimum target, and aspect policy.

competencies.statement: Handle configure events with bounded coalescing and redraw only from committed current dimensions.

competencies.statement: Test Tk scaling, font metrics, line widths, focus indicators, and platform display settings instead of assuming raw pixels.

competencies.statement: Provide a usable minimum, scroll or alternate representation, and reversible recovery when the visual grid cannot fit.
misconceptions: Clipping controls is acceptable for unusually small windows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide a usable minimum, scroll or alternate representation, and reversible recovery when the visual grid cannot fit.

competencies.statement: Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes.
misconceptions: Each device event should directly mutate maze state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes.

competencies.statement: Choose widget, class, toplevel, tag, and bindtag scope while preserving expected default behavior.
misconceptions: bind always appends a handler without affecting existing bindings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose widget, class, toplevel, tag, and bindtag scope while preserving expected default behavior.

competencies.statement: Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.
misconceptions: Canvas keyboard control works whenever the mouse is over it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.

competencies.statement: Give pointer-only operations keyboard or single-action alternatives without imposing holds, repeats, or precise timing.
misconceptions: A clickable cell is accessible because every desktop has a mouse.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give pointer-only operations keyboard or single-action alternatives without imposing holds, repeats, or precise timing.

competencies.statement: Record action, source, focus owner, prior state, accepted transition, feedback, and changed binding evidence.
misconceptions: A keypress log alone proves the intended action occurred.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record action, source, focus owner, prior state, accepted transition, feedback, and changed binding evidence.

competencies.statement: Map each cell to a vertex and each reciprocal open passage to an undirected edge without leaking render state.
misconceptions: Every neighboring cell pair is a graph edge even when a wall separates them.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map each cell to a vertex and each reciprocal open passage to an undirected edge without leaking render state.

competencies.statement: Use traversal evidence to count connected components and produce a witness for an unreachable cell.
misconceptions: An entrance and exit path proves every cell is connected.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use traversal evidence to count connected components and produce a witness for an unreachable cell.

competencies.statement: Detect or construct a cycle witness rather than infer cycles from edge count alone on an unvalidated graph.
misconceptions: Any graph with vertex count minus one edges is a tree.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect or construct a cycle witness rather than infer cycles from edge count alone on an unvalidated graph.

competencies.statement: Establish connectedness plus acyclicity or the equivalent finite undirected edge condition before calling a maze perfect.
misconceptions: Perfect means visually difficult rather than graph-theoretically unique-path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Establish connectedness plus acyclicity or the equivalent finite undirected edge condition before calling a maze perfect.

competencies.statement: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.
misconceptions: Algorithm complexity can be reported without defining graph representation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.

competencies.statement: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.
misconceptions: Randomly removing walls eventually creates a valid maze.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.

competencies.statement: Enumerate in-bounds unvisited neighbors before randomized selection and retain deterministic ordering before shuffling.
misconceptions: A set iteration order plus a seed is reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enumerate in-bounds unvisited neighbors before randomized selection and retain deterministic ordering before shuffling.

competencies.statement: Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.
misconceptions: Calling seed once makes every future run independently reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.

competencies.statement: Compare recursive and explicit-stack backtracking for clarity, depth, pause, serialization, and failure behavior.
misconceptions: Recursion is the algorithm while a stack would change its result.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare recursive and explicit-stack backtracking for clarity, depth, pause, serialization, and failure behavior.

competencies.statement: Verify all cells visited, edge count, reciprocal walls, connectivity, acyclicity, seed replay, and changed dimensions.
misconceptions: Reaching the initial cell again proves generation completed correctly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify all cells visited, edge count, reciprocal walls, connectivity, acyclicity, seed replay, and changed dimensions.

competencies.statement: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.
misconceptions: Randomized Prim is the same procedure and distribution as depth-first backtracking.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.

competencies.statement: Shuffle candidate walls and use disjoint sets to admit only edges joining different components.
misconceptions: Kruskal needs to check graph connectivity after every accepted wall.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Shuffle candidate walls and use disjoint sets to admit only edges joining different components.

competencies.statement: Construct a uniform spanning tree with loop-erased random walks and state termination assumptions.
misconceptions: Every randomized spanning-tree algorithm samples trees uniformly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct a uniform spanning tree with loop-erased random walks and state termination assumptions.

competencies.statement: Measure corridor length, turns, dead ends, path stretch, branching, and distributions across many recorded seeds.
misconceptions: One maze screenshot establishes algorithm bias and player difficulty.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure corridor length, turns, dead ends, path stretch, branching, and distributions across many recorded seeds.

competencies.statement: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.
misconceptions: The fastest generator is automatically the best game design.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.

competencies.statement: Define start, goal, passable-neighbor order, visited policy, result, and failure contract before traversal.
misconceptions: DFS behavior is fully defined by saying search recursively.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define start, goal, passable-neighbor order, visited policy, result, and failure contract before traversal.

competencies.statement: Mark a vertex when discovered so cycles and converging paths cannot schedule repeated work.
misconceptions: Marking only after exploring a cell is equivalent on every maze.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Mark a vertex when discovered so cycles and converging paths cannot schedule repeated work.

competencies.statement: Separate explored vertices, active route, rejected branches, and final start-to-goal witness.
misconceptions: The visited set is the solution path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate explored vertices, active route, rejected branches, and final start-to-goal witness.

competencies.statement: Bound or replace recursion for deep mazes and report failure without leaving partial UI state.
misconceptions: Grid size alone guarantees recursion will remain safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound or replace recursion for deep mazes and report failure without leaving partial UI state.

competencies.statement: Verify valid adjacent steps, no wall crossing, correct endpoints, termination, unsolvable behavior, and non-optimality limits.
misconceptions: Finding any path proves a shortest path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify valid adjacent steps, no wall crossing, correct endpoints, termination, unsolvable behavior, and non-optimality limits.

competencies.statement: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.
misconceptions: BFS is DFS with a different variable name.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.

competencies.statement: Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.
misconceptions: Removing index zero from a list has constant cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.

competencies.statement: Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.
misconceptions: A predecessor may be replaced freely without changing shortest-path proof.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.

competencies.statement: Explain why BFS minimizes edge count only when every admitted passage has equal cost.
misconceptions: BFS always finds the cheapest route through any maze.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain why BFS minimizes edge count only when every admitted passage has equal cost.

competencies.statement: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.
misconceptions: One perfect maze is enough to test BFS.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.

competencies.statement: Define nonnegative passage costs, units, blocked edges, accumulated route cost, and the player meaning of weight.
misconceptions: Adding decorative terrain colors creates a weighted graph automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define nonnegative passage costs, units, blocked edges, accumulated route cost, and the player meaning of weight.

competencies.statement: Relax an edge only through a lower tentative cost and finalize vertices under the nonnegative-weight contract.
misconceptions: Dijkstra can safely handle negative passage costs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relax an edge only through a lower tentative cost and finalize vertices under the nonnegative-weight contract.

competencies.statement: Push improved priorities and reject stale heap entries by comparing popped and recorded costs.
misconceptions: heapq updates the priority of an existing tuple automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Push improved priorities and reject stale heap entries by comparing popped and recorded costs.

competencies.statement: Choose and scale an admissible, consistent heuristic for the actual movement and cost model.
misconceptions: Manhattan distance is valid regardless of diagonal moves or minimum edge cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose and scale an admissible, consistent heuristic for the actual movement and cost model.

competencies.statement: Compare route validity, total cost, expansions, frontier peak, heuristic zero case, unreachable case, and changed weights.
misconceptions: Fewer expanded cells proves A-star returned the optimal route.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare route validity, total cost, expansions, frontier peak, heuristic zero case, unreachable case, and changed weights.

competencies.statement: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
misconceptions: A solver returning true is self-authenticating evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.

competencies.statement: State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
misconceptions: Every search implementation is complete because the maze is finite.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.

competencies.statement: Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
misconceptions: The first goal added to a frontier always establishes optimality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.

competencies.statement: Compare algorithms on identical recorded mazes with warm-up, repeated trials, separated generation time, and distributions.

competencies.statement: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

misconceptions: An algorithm must know animation delay to be visualized.

competencies.statement: Schedule one future callback with after and retain its identifier and owning run generation.
misconceptions: after(delay, callback) blocks until the callback completes.

competencies.statement: Pause without losing algorithm state, resume once, and apply changed speed to future turns predictably.
misconceptions: Changing a speed variable updates callbacks already queued.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pause without losing algorithm state, resume once, and apply changed speed to future turns predictably.

competencies.statement: Cancel pending callbacks and reject late work using a run token during reset, close, or new generation.
misconceptions: Destroying visible items prevents stale callbacks from running.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cancel pending callbacks and reject late work using a run token during reset, close, or new generation.

competencies.statement: Verify callback count, state order, pause stability, changed delay, cancellation, close behavior, and native event timing.
misconceptions: A smooth recording proves callback ownership and cancellation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify callback count, state order, pause stability, changed delay, cancellation, close behavior, and native event timing.

competencies.statement: Define idle, generating, ready, solving, paused, completed, cancelled, failed, and closing states with owned data.
misconceptions: Two booleans can represent every meaningful application state clearly.

competencies.statement: Admit commands through an explicit current-state transition table with effects and rejection feedback.
misconceptions: Disabling buttons is enough to prevent invalid programmatic transitions.

competencies.statement: Derive enabled controls, labels, focus targets, status, and progress from state rather than mutating them independently.

misconceptions: Unexpected errors should terminate the desktop process immediately.

competencies.statement: Cover every allowed and rejected edge, repeated command, cancellation race, failure, and close transition.
misconceptions: Testing the happy path visits every meaningful state.

misconceptions: Passing the root object everywhere is simpler than interfaces.

misconceptions: Saving only the random seed reproduces every future maze session.

misconceptions: Recorded callback timestamps are the authoritative game logic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Replay logical commands and algorithm steps independently from wall-clock animation timing.

competencies.statement: Validate version, types, bounds, enums, lengths, unknown fields, and cross-field invariants before constructing state.
misconceptions: json.loads returns trustworthy application objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate version, types, bounds, enums, lengths, unknown fields, and cross-field invariants before constructing state.

competencies.statement: Write or replace state through a controlled adapter that preserves the last known good save on failure.
misconceptions: Writing directly over the only save is adequate for local software.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write or replace state through a controlled adapter that preserves the last known good save on failure.

competencies.statement: Migrate supported versions explicitly, reject unsupported or corrupted data safely, and retain recovery evidence.
misconceptions: Adding defaults silently makes every old save compatible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Migrate supported versions explicitly, reject unsupported or corrupted data safely, and retain recovery evidence.

competencies.statement: Separate pure unit, property, mutation, integration, Tcl, Tk widget, display, accessibility, package, and player tests.
misconceptions: A headless Tk test replaces real display and assistive-technology testing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate pure unit, property, mutation, integration, Tcl, Tk widget, display, accessibility, package, and player tests.

competencies.statement: Generate bounded grids and assert reciprocal walls, connectivity, tree rules, route validity, cost, determinism, and state invariants.
misconceptions: Many random examples prove an invariant without an independent oracle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate bounded grids and assert reciprocal walls, connectivity, tree rules, route validity, cost, determinism, and state invariants.

competencies.statement: Introduce deliberate wall, queue, heuristic, cancellation, focus, and schema defects and prove named tests fail causally.
misconceptions: Passing tests demonstrate they would catch a realistic regression.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Introduce deliberate wall, queue, heuristic, cancellation, focus, and schema defects and prove named tests fail causally.

competencies.statement: Use pure fakes and Tcl-only checks where valid, then run Tk and display tests under a controlled platform matrix.
misconceptions: Creating Tcl without Tk proves Canvas, focus, fonts, and bindings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use pure fakes and Tcl-only checks where valid, then run Tk and display tests under a controlled platform matrix.

competencies.statement: Define representative tasks, accommodations, observation boundaries, barrier severity, consent, privacy, and closure evidence.
misconceptions: Watching one developer solve a maze is a learner usability study.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define representative tasks, accommodations, observation boundaries, barrier severity, consent, privacy, and closure evidence.

competencies.statement: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.
misconceptions: Every grid algorithm is linear because the screen is two-dimensional.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.

competencies.statement: Measure generation, solving, state conversion, drawing, callback delay, startup, save, and package behavior separately.
misconceptions: One end-to-end timer identifies the bottleneck.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure generation, solving, state conversion, drawing, callback delay, startup, save, and package behavior separately.

competencies.statement: Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.
misconceptions: High average frame rate guarantees responsive controls.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.

competencies.statement: Reduce item churn, batch or diff changes, bound annotations, and retest visual and accessibility correctness.
misconceptions: A faster Canvas item count is automatically equivalent output.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reduce item churn, batch or diff changes, bound annotations, and retest visual and accessibility correctness.

competencies.statement: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.
misconceptions: If a large maze eventually completes it is supported.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.

competencies.statement: Define package layout, metadata, Python requirement, dependencies, console or GUI entry point, and import-safe startup.
misconceptions: A desktop script needs no package metadata or entry point.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define package layout, metadata, Python requirement, dependencies, console or GUI entry point, and import-safe startup.

competencies.statement: Load licensed package resources without assuming source checkout or current working directory layout.
misconceptions: Relative paths beside the source file work unchanged in every bundle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Load licensed package resources without assuming source checkout or current working directory layout.

competencies.statement: Inspect PyInstaller analysis, tkinter hooks, Tcl/Tk libraries and data, hidden imports, warnings, and artifact contents.
misconceptions: PyInstaller success proves every runtime dependency was discovered.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect PyInstaller analysis, tkinter hooks, Tcl/Tk libraries and data, hidden imports, warnings, and artifact contents.

competencies.statement: Produce and test separate artifacts on each supported operating system and architecture with recorded toolchains.
misconceptions: PyInstaller cross-compiles portable desktop executables from one host.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce and test separate artifacts on each supported operating system and architecture with recorded toolchains.

competencies.statement: Launch from a clean user account or machine, exercise create-solve-save-recover-close, and retain artifact identity and diagnostics.
misconceptions: Running from the dist directory on the build host is a clean-install test.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Launch from a clean user account or machine, exercise create-solve-save-recover-close, and retain artifact identity and diagnostics.

competencies.statement: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.
misconceptions: Offline desktop software has no meaningful attack surface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.

competencies.statement: Use bounded data-only formats and reject pickle or executable object reconstruction for untrusted saves.
misconceptions: A local pickle is safe because the learner created the file.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use bounded data-only formats and reject pickle or executable object reconstruction for untrusted saves.

competencies.statement: Validate dimensions, cell counts, edge counts, nesting, string length, numeric ranges, and total work before allocation.
misconceptions: Catching MemoryError is a sufficient size policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate dimensions, cell counts, edge counts, nesting, string length, numeric ranges, and total work before allocation.

competencies.statement: Keep imported data and package resources within explicit authorities and avoid user-controlled executable lookup.
misconceptions: Joining a chosen filename to a safe directory prevents every escape.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep imported data and package resources within explicit authorities and avoid user-controlled executable lookup.

competencies.statement: Collect minimal redacted diagnostics with consent, bounded retention, export control, and deletion while preserving causal evidence.
misconceptions: Verbose logs are harmless because they stay on the same computer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Collect minimal redacted diagnostics with consent, bounded retention, export control, and deletion while preserving causal evidence.

competencies.statement: Require schema, order, duplication, source, model, algorithm, native GUI, accessibility, performance, security, package, and learner-flow gates.
misconceptions: Thousands of generated learning steps establish release readiness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require schema, order, duplication, source, model, algorithm, native GUI, accessibility, performance, security, package, and learner-flow gates.

competencies.statement: Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.
misconceptions: A new version only needs to open its own newly created state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.

competencies.statement: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration.
misconceptions: Restarting the program is adequate recovery evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rehearse corrupted state, cancelled work, missing resources, display failure, bad release, rollback, and last-good restoration.

competencies.statement: Publish accessible controls, known limitations, diagnostics, safe reset, data location, support path, and accountable owners.
misconceptions: Self-explanatory software needs no support documentation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish accessible controls, known limitations, diagnostics, safe reset, data location, support path, and accountable owners.

competencies.statement: Defend player value, algorithms, accessibility, correctness, performance, security, packaging, recovery, and residual risk together.
misconceptions: A correct solver is sufficient evidence for a production learning project.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend player value, algorithms, accessibility, correctness, performance, security, packaging, recovery, and residual risk together.

credential.title: Build and Ship an Accessible Maze Solver with Python 3.14 and Tkinter Certificate
